channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Trump orders new round of strikes after calling Iranian leaders ‘scum’
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Victoria Craw, Dan Lamothe
item.description: The president previewed the attack earlier in the day, later adding that any action would end quickly and suggesting the U.S. may reimpose a naval blockade.

item.title: In shift, Trump praises Zelensky, will let Ukraine build Patriot missiles
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Cleve R. Wootson Jr.
item.description: It was a dramatic departure from Trump’s more acerbic tone toward Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, whom he once derided as ungrateful.

item.title: In Iraq, huge crowds mourning Iranian leader reveal limits of U.S. role
item.dc:creator: Mustafa Salim
item.description: Some officials said the outpouring of grief was intended to show the continuing close ties between Iraq and Iran despite U.S. efforts to hold sway in Baghdad.

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
item.dc:creator: Dan Lamothe, Victoria Craw
item.description: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Foreign media were granted rare visas to cover Khamenei’s funeral. Here’s what our reporter saw.
item.dc:creator: Susannah George
item.description: A regime that has survived months of attack by the United States and Israel is projecting a show of strength with enormous crowds and the reappearance of military leaders.

item.title: Trump’s NATO-bashing casts shadow over U.S. pledge to defend allies
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis
item.description: NATO’s Article 5 has long promised that an attack against one is an attack against all. President Donald Trump is not so sure.

item.title: U.S. gave Tehran details on Iranian asylum seekers, lawsuit alleges
item.dc:creator: Adam Taylor
item.description: The claim, outlined in court papers, contends information shared with Iran could jeopardize the lives of pro-democracy protesters, religious minorities and LGTBQ people.

item.title: Haitians in U.S. fear mass deportations; Haiti fears mass return
item.dc:creator: Widlore Mérancourt
item.description: Mass deportations would cost the economy billions of dollars, analysts say, and mass arrivals would challenge a country already suffering record displacement.

item.title: Belarus gets squeezed as Putin seeks war help and Ukraine threatens strikes
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: Ukraine’s northern neighbor was used as a staging ground for Russia’s 2022 invasion, but the country seems intent on not being drawn further into the Kremlin’s war.

item.title: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
item.dc:creator: Cate Cadell
item.description: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.
